pageDescriptionAfter: Use our free Newton meter to foot-pound converter with formula and precision control.
hintDirection: Choose whether you are converting metric Nm to imperial ft-lbs or the reverse.
hintValue: Enter the torque reading from your wrench, manual, or spec sheet (0 or greater).
hintPrecision: Match the precision shown on your torque wrench or service manual.
importantBody: Always use the torque specification from your vehicle or equipment manual. This converter is for unit conversion only — it does not replace manufacturer torque values. When in doubt, tighten to the lower end of a range and use a calibrated torque wrench.
resultsSubtitle: Based on your inputs and the standard Nm ↔ ft-lbs formula
emptyHint: Enter a torque value and click “Convert torque” to see your result here.
formulaRefNote: Standard conversion factors used by this calculator:
proTip1: European and Asian manuals list torque in Nm; US wrenches often read ft-lbs — convert before you tighten.
proTip2: For critical fasteners (wheels, head bolts), use a calibrated torque wrench, not impact tools alone.
proTip3: If a spec gives a range (e.g. 80–100 Nm), convert both ends and stay within the converted range.
proTip4: Spark plugs and small fasteners need lower torque; over-tightening strips threads easily.
proTip5: Store your wrench at the lowest setting after use to preserve calibration.
errValue: Enter a valid number (0 or greater).
